ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Report On What Is Being Done To Enrich The Conservatee'S Life
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Off Calendar

Petition For Authorization To Pay Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Michael Tuason (Petitioner/Trustee).

The Court made reference to the declaration that was filed, stated that it is not satisfied that enough is being explored as to what can be done to enrich the Conservatee's life and to benefit her, suggested that the Trustee look into Silver Rides so that she can go to the beach or the park, and also suggested that the Trustee look into different forms of therapy. The Petition for Authorization to Pay Attorneys Fees is taken under submission. The Court set a six month status hearing of June 4, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 206 for filing of a report on what is being done to enrich the Conservatee's life.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Z. Grimaldi. Not reported.

Petition For Authorization To Pay Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Continued To Nov-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authorization To Pay Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Oct-02-2017 Continued To Nov-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Michael Tuason (Trustee).

The Court informed Counsel that a declaration is needed regarding possible services that can be provided to the Conservatee (Ms. Padilla) to enrich her life. Mr. Conrad stated that most of the legal services had to do with an inquiry of Court staff as to why Ms. Padilla was still receiving benefits, that they looked into what Ms. Padilla would need to better her life about a year and a half ago, that the Trustee felt that all of her needs were being met very well at her placement and that he will submit a declaration as requested. Mr. Conrad also stated that they have looked into what they could spend more money on, that he went to Social Security and reported that Ms. Padilla had money, that Social Security did nothing about it and that he spent three hours in a conference with Ms. Hanowksy brainstorming on what could be done. The Petition for Authorization to Pay Attorney's Fees is continued to November 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Authorization To Pay Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Continued To Oct-02-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authorization To Pay Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Hanowsky for petitioner Michael Tuason.

The Court stated that issues have been raised, and the Court has received a response. He asked Ms. Hanowsky if she had anything to add to her response. She stated that this was an old case and had taken a lot of time to review and prepare. The Court responded that it was not so much an issue of time as the matters for which charges were sought, including faulty e-filings, and the appropriate rate for the services. Counsel explained the extent of services provided/time spent to support her fee request. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported).

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipts For The Blocked Accounts
Off Calendar Dec-02-2013 Continued To Jan-06-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elisabeth Hanowsky for Roman Tuason and Michael Tuason (Co-Trustees).

Ms. Hanowksy stated that they have not deposited the funds into the blocked accounts and requested more time stating that the sale closed 10-days ago as of Friday. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts was continued to January 6, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Permanent Court Supervision And For Confirmation Of Co-Trustees
Off Calendar Dec-13-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Roman Tuason, Jr.

Mr. Conrad explained that the real property was left vacant because the Conservatee for many years expressed wishes to return home, provided a copy of an order in the conservatorship matter (255054) dated August 27, 2002 authorizing the Conservators to keep the property vacant, stated that he notified Social Security of the income (sale proceeds) and that he is working with Social Security to preserve benefits. Mr. Conrad also stated that that there was no economic activity since 2007, suggested starting the accounting with 2013, that they don't need an accounting, that the funds will go into a blocked account, that they are only requesting reimbursement to Mr. Tuason for expenses of the property and that he can attach a copy of the escrow statement to the petition. The Court stated that the Trust should remain under Court supervision, that no accounting will be required as long as the funds remain in a blocked account and that Mr. Tuason may petition for reimbursement of expenses for the property. The Court granted the Petition for Order Bringing Trust Under Permanent Court Supervision (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Permanent Court Supervision And For Confirmation Of Co-Trustees
Off Calendar Nov-05-2013 Continued To Nov-19-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Executed Declaration Of Trust, Lourdes T. Padilla 2007 Revocable Living Trust
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (Report Of Sale And Petition For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property)
Off Calendar Nov-06-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Roamn Tuason and Michael Tuason.

Mr. Conrad stated that he spoke with Attorney Brent Kato and that Mr. Kato had no objection to the petition being granted. The Court informed Counsel that placing the funds into a blocked account will not provide the protection he was seeking with regard to the Beneficiary's eligibility for public benefits. The Court also informed Counsel that an accounting petition will need to be filed in order for the request for reimbursement of expenses in the amount of $47,800.00 to be addressed. In addition, the Court raised the issue of establishing a Special Needs Trust. The sale was confirmed at $551,200.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt and review of a revised order). The proceeds from the sale are to be deposited into two blocked accounts in lieu of bond. The revised order is to name two different financial institutions. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts was set for December 2, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Ex Parte Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision And For Confirmation Of Co-Trustees Pending Hearing On Permanent Court Supervision
Off Calendar Aug-19-2013 Granted Per Written Order
